image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: You can't see a black hole — so how did scientists capture its shadow?
Context visual: Meet the Event Horizon Telescope—an array of global telescopes acting as one gigantic eye.
Escalation visual: In 2019, they unveiled the first ever image of a black hole: M87*, showcasing its photon ring.
Evidence visual: This shadow isn’t the black hole itself, but the light bending around it—a cosmic illusion.
Payoff visual: The EHT Collaboration's groundbreaking work pushed the boundaries of our understanding of the universe.
Cta visual: Curious about what else we can find in space? Hit that follow button for more!
